Charlie Brown walks up to Lucy and greets her by
saying, "Happy New Year, Lucy!".
.sp
(The background is a grass field.)
.sp
(He has his arm extended as a sign of greeting.)
.sp
Lucy asks,"Does that make it so?".
.sp
(Charlie Brown has look of puzzlement and interest.)
.sp
Lucy continues, "Does your saying, 'Happy New Year'
MAKE it happy?".
.sp
(Lucy's arms are upraised as sign of emphasis.)
.sp
Lucy goes on, "Just because you SAY it does that
mean it WILL be?".
.sp
(Lucy's arms are outstretched as sign of question.)
.sp
(Charlie Brown has a facial expression of confusion.)
.sp
Lucy shouts, "IS THIS A GUARANTEE?
IS THIS ..."
.sp
Charlie Brown says, "Oh, good grief".
.sp
(He has his Charlie Brown expres4/$sion)
.pa
.ce
A Possible Parse
.sp
 (Background grass-field)
.sp
 (key-frame1
     (CB (walking (towards Lucy))
	 (says "Happy New Year, Lucy!)
	 (greeting Lucy)
	 (arm extended)) ; implied by the above
     (Lucy (standing (facing CB))))
 (key-frame2
     (CB (standing (facing Lucy)))
     (Lucy (asks "Does that make it so?")))
 (key-frame3
     (CB (arm hand-on-chin)
	 (expression (and interest puzzlement)))
     (Lucy (arms up-stretched)
	   (expression emphatic)
	   (asks "Does ... ?")))
 (key-frame4
     (CB (expression confusion))
     (Lucy (asks "Just because ...")
	   (arms out-stretched)
	   (expression questioning)))
 (key-frame5
     (CB ())
     (Lucy (shouts "IS THIS A GUARANTEE?")))
 (key-frame6
     (CB (expression good-grief)
	 (says "Oh, good grief"))
     (Lucy (continues "IS THIS ...")))
.pa
.ce
Fills in Key-frames
.sp 2
 (key-frame1
     (CB (walking (towards Lucy))
	 (says "Happy New Year, Lucy!)
	 (greeting Lucy)
	 (arm extended)
	 <<expression mildly-friendly>>
		;since greeting is going on
	 <<position screen-left>>
		; pretty arbitrary
	 <<orientation full-right-profile>>
		 ;based on above position and
		 ;"(walking (towards Lucy))"
	 <<clothes default-cb>>
	 ;a smarter version would know that
	 ;"Happy New Year ===> (clother winter))
     (Lucy (standing (facing CB))
	   <<expression mild>>
	;either default or for contrast
	;with  future frames
	   <<arms down>>; standard default
	   <<position screen-right>>
	; since there are only 2 people
	; and CB is "screen-left"
	   <<orientation full-left-profile>>
	; since facing CB
	   <<clothes default-lucy>>))
.pa
  (key-frame2
     (CB (standing (facing Lucy))
	 <<arms down>>)
	; people get tired of extending their
	; arms if nothing to the contrary
	; indicated. the rest is "no-change"
     (Lucy (asks "Does that make it so?")))
	; the rest is "no-change"
.sp 2
.ce
Differences Between Key-frame1 and Key-frame2
.sp 2
 (Transformation key-frame1 key-frame2
 	(CB (walking standing)
	; assumes "facing" and "towards"
	; are close enough
	    (arms (one extended) down)
	; note there is no position
	; change except implicit
	; in "walking"
		 (talking listening))
	(Lucy (listening talking)))
.pa
.ce
Two examples of a difference being run
.sp 2
	(arms (one extended) (both down))
 is sent to "arms" specialist resulting in:
.sp
 (body-part1 right-arm
  ; since most people are right handed
   (body-orientation full-right-profile)
   (orientation +30))
.sp
 (body-part2 right-arm
	     (orientation -90))
.sp
 (transformation right-arm
		 (orientation 30 -90))
.sp
which combined with a time constraint of say 14 frames results
in calls to draw-arm with orientations of say,
.sp
 (30 26 20 8 -4 -16 -28 -40
 		 -52 -64 -76  -80 -86 -90)
.sp
 Note the "ease-in" and "ease-out"
.pa
Suppose that one of the differences was
  (CB (expression happy neutral))
.br
Then the "expression" expert would return
 (body-part1 head
	     (orientation full-front)
	     (mouth smiling)
	     (eyes happy))
	;could have ears, nose, etc. too
 (body-part2 head
	     (orientation full-front)
	     (mouth dash)
	     (eyes normal))
 (transformation head
		 (mouth (smiling dash))
		 (eyes (happy normal)))
.sp
Then the system recurses on "mouth" and "eyes". For "mouth"
 (body-part1 mouth
	     (head-orientation full-front)
	     (length 100) ; in turtle units ?
	     (curvature 120)) ; in terms of sum
			      ; of RT and LT
 (body-part2 mouth
 	     (head-orientation full-front)
	     (length 50)
	     (curvature 0))
 (transformation mouth
		 (length 100 50)
		 (curvature 120 0))
.br
It could be that "length" and "curvature" are interpolated
in parallel or there may be advise otherwise.
.pa
.ds
.ce
Examples of Advise and Criticism from User

 "No, have CB come in from screen right"

 "Lucy turned her head too fast."
 "We should zoom in on Lucy when she shouts"
 "Half way through they should turn towards the "audience"."
 "Something is wrong in the last scene but I don't know which"
 "Instead of first cut try a fast dissolve."

	When should shadow appear under chin?

B to C ---- When does other eye appear?
.sp
C to E When should back hair disappear?
.sp
C to F ---- Is this mostly shifting ear, eyes, nose, and
mouth to until the centered?
.sp
Can B, C, and D be made to look reasonable? If not
can they have only a few frames (i.e. go by quickly)
and never have action STOP on them.
.end
